label: Sweetness
description: Perceived sugar and richness on the palate, from bone-dry to dessert-like.

label: Caramel
description: Toffee, butterscotch and browned-sugar notes, often from charred oak.

label: Honey
description: Floral-sweet honeyed character, common in Speyside malts and Sauternes-finished spirits.

label: Vanilla
description: Vanillin extracted from new oak; the signature of American barrels.

label: Fruit
description: Orchard, tropical or dark fruit — apples, cherries, mango, raisin, plum.

label: Citrus
description: Lemon, orange, grapefruit brightness; key in gin, wheat beers and young wines.

label: Floral
description: Blossom, rose, violet and elderflower aromatics.

label: Herbal
description: Grass, mint, pine, tea and botanical herb notes.

label: Chocolate
description: Cocoa and dark chocolate depth, typical of stouts, sherried whisky and aged rum.

label: Coffee
description: Roasted coffee and espresso character from dark malts or heavy char.

label: Nut
description: Almond, hazelnut, walnut — hallmarks of oloroso sherry and tawny port.

label: Leather
description: Aged, rancio, tobacco-and-leather complexity that comes with long maturation.

label: Earthy
description: Soil, mushroom, agave roastiness, funk and minerality.

label: Oak
description: Woody structure and tannin from barrel maturation.

label: Smoke
description: Peat smoke, mesquite, bonfire and ash — Islay's calling card.

label: Spice
description: Baking spice: cinnamon, clove, nutmeg, ginger.

label: Pepper
description: Black or chile pepper heat, rye kick and alcohol warmth.

label: Body
description: Weight and texture on the palate, from light and crisp to thick and chewy.

label: Finish
description: How long the flavor persists after swallowing.

label: Complexity
description: How many distinct layers unfold as you taste.
